title: saponaria prostrata subsp. calvertii
intro: Saponaria prostrata subsp. calvertii, also known as the Alpine Soapwort or Saponaria calvertii, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carnation family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Saponaria prostrata subsp. calvertii can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and creams, which can be used topically to treat various skin conditions.
